snapshot.scope: Independent Planning review binds machine-local Permission Settings, revision-fenced YOLO, first-party read-only filesystem/network adapters, durable Job authority and model-egress reauthorization to the permission, settings, API, architecture, security and trace owners on origin/main aac6b47.

reviewers.task: Permission Settings and read-only Node OS capability contract-impact review
reviewers.independence: Product Planning did not author the capability adapters and independently compared permission revision authority, protected resources, durable Job binding, model-egress reauthorization and renderer disclosure boundaries with owner contracts and trace evidence.

dimensions.summary: Existing Permission, Settings, API, Architecture and Security owners carry the capability behavior without introducing a duplicate owner document or granting renderer-side Node authority.
Owner text agrees that explicit YOLO is revision fenced, protected roots and raw credentials remain blocked, read-only tools bind to durable Jobs, and untrusted results face model-egress review.
Exact tests narrow the Permission and E8.03 gaps without promotion; semantic claims retain planned status and enumerate saved grants, mutating tools, UI, Operations and platform residuals.
Focused regressions cover token replay, revision drift, protected roots, symlinks, SSRF, bounded output, cancellation, durable automatic decisions and restricted-result egress blocking.
One bounded durable Decision plus existing subject owners and trace projections carry the implementation delta without a new owner document or duplicated permission policy.

verification.command: Independent implementation and owner-contract review of Permission Settings and read-only Node OS capabilities based on origin/main aac6b47
verification.evidence: Review covers Main permission/capability implementation, executable schemas, focused tests and affected owner/trace projections; raw paths, credentials and permission internals remain excluded from renderer IPC.

verification.command: npm run verify:docs && npm run verify:decisions && npm run verify:traceability
verification.evidence: Links, whitespace, document map, PRD structure, decision records and narrowed Permission requirement mappings pass after bounded owner synchronization.

verification.command: npm run verify:contracts && npm run verify:independent-documentation-review && npm run verify:documentation-leanness
verification.evidence: CON-001 through CON-004 remain green and the recipe binds governed roots plus changed permission/capability code and tests without Requirement, Exit or Phase promotion.

coordination.taskTitle: Permission Settings and read-only Node OS capability synchronization
coordination.compatibility: The candidate adds explicit machine-local authority and bounded read-only tools without ambient renderer access; existing vault tools, stronger confirmation layers and release scope remain unchanged.
coordination.acknowledgement: Product Planning synchronized permission, settings, API, architecture, security and trace owners; this review does not claim saved-grant creation, write/delete/shell/package tools, visible UI or signed-platform acceptance.

nonBlockingRisks: Saved scoped grants can be inspected and revoked but are not yet created by a production authorization flow.
External write, delete, shell, package and arbitrary Node execution remain unavailable pending typed effect-specific owners and recovery semantics.
The polished Permission Settings renderer remains unbound until its exact UI-owned adapter is mechanically integrated.
Windows and signed packaged permission/capability evidence remain open, including OS-specific protected resources and cancellation behavior.
Operation visibility and release-wide permission history acceptance remain open; no Requirement, Exit or Phase status is promoted.
